engineGuide.h2: Ford Territory Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Ford Territory across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 0L Diesel
entries.history: The 2.0L Bi-Turbo diesel was introduced as part of the Territory SZ facelift in 2014. This engine offered improved fuel economy while maintaining competitive torque figures for the large SUV segment.
compatibleVariants: SZ MkII TX Diesel, SZ MkII TS Diesel, SZ MkII Titanium Diesel
entries.cta: Get quotes for CAGDA Ford Territory engine replacement

entries.title: 0L Bi-Turbo Diesel (Chinese Market)
entries.history: The second-generation Territory (CX743) was produced in China for select markets including Australia and the Middle East. It shared some diesel powertrain components with European Ford models.
compatibleVariants: CX743 TX Diesel, CX743 TS Diesel, CX743 Titanium Diesel
entries.cta: Get quotes for CAGDA Chinese Ford Territory engine replacement

entries.title: 0L Petrol
entries.history: The legendary Australian-designed Barra 190 is a 4.0L DOHC 24-valve inline-6 known for exceptional reliability. It powers the majority of SX and SY Territory models and remains the most common engine in the Australian domestic market.
compatibleVariants: SX TX, SX TS, SX Ghia, SY TX, SY TS, SY Ghia, SY MkII TX, SY MkII TS, SY MkII Ghia, SZ TX Petrol, SZ TS Petrol, SZ Titanium Petrol
commonFailures: Timing chain stretch (hydraulic tensioner failure, rattling noise on cold start)
VCT phaser failure (rattles and poor low-end performance)
entries.cta: Get quotes for Barra 190 Ford Territory engine replacement

entries.title: 0L Petrol
entries.history: Early SX Territory models featured the Barra 182, producing slightly less power than the later Barra 190. This engine shares the same robust architecture and reliability characteristics as its more powerful successor.
compatibleVariants: SX TX (early production), SX TS (early production), SX Ghia (early production)
commonFailures: Coil pack failure leading to misfire
Oil leaks from rocker cover gasket
entries.cta: Get quotes for Barra 182 Ford Territory engine replacement

entries.title: 0L EcoBoost Petrol
entries.history: The 2.0L EcoBoost joined the Territory range with the 2014 SZ facelift. This turbocharged direct-injection petrol engine offered improved fuel economy over the Barra while maintaining competitive power output.
compatibleVariants: SZ MkII TX EcoBoost, SZ MkII TS EcoBoost, SZ MkII Titanium EcoBoost
commonFailures: Carbon buildup on intake valves (direct injection)
entries.cta: Get quotes for CAFDD Ford Territory engine replacement

entries.title: 5L EcoBoost Petrol
entries.history: The second-generation Territory received a 1.8L GTDi petrol engine in the 2025 facelift. This turbocharged direct-injection four-cylinder delivers 188hp and represents the modern direction for the nameplate.
compatibleVariants: CX743 TX EcoBoost, CX743 TS EcoBoost, CX743 Titanium EcoBoost
commonFailures: Coolant loss from water pump seal
Early reports of direct-injection carbon buildup typical of EcoBoost engines
entries.cta: Get quotes for 4G18F6E Ford Territory engine replacement

entries.title: Hybrid (select Asian markets only)
entries.cta: Contact UK specialists for import-specific hybrid engine queries

commonProblems.h2: Ford Territory Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense

problems.h4: Barra 4.0L Timing Chain Failure - Why Territory Petrol Engines Rattle on Start-Up
problems.affectedModels: Ford Territory SX, SY, SY MkII, SZ (2004-2016, Barra 190 & Barra 182)
problems.rootCause: The Barra uses timing chains with hydraulic tensioners (not belts). Over time, oil pressure drops or tensioner seals fail, allowing chain slack. This leads to chain slap against guides, eventual guide breakage, and skipped timing.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing chains, tensioners, guides, and water pump while accessible
repairOptions.longevity: 100,000+ miles if done before chain slap damages guides

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of fully rebuilt Barra 190/182 with upgraded tensioners
repairOptions.longevity: New engine with 12-month warranty; best value if chain has jumped

problems.recommendation: If you catch the rattle early (cold start only, no check engine light), the preventative chain replacement is the smart move on a Territory worth £3,000+. If the engine has thrown P0016 (cam/crank correlation) or runs poorly, skip the repair - the labour cost to inspect and replace damaged valves isn’t worth it. Go straight to a rebuilt Barra with upgraded components.
problems.cta: Compare Barra 4.0L timing chain repair vs replacement prices for your Territory

problems.h4: 2.7 TDV6 (276DT) Oil Pump & Crankshaft Failure - Territory Diesel & Land Rover Donor Engines
problems.affectedModels: Ford Territory SZ (2011-2016, 2.7L V6 Diesel 276DT) - also fitted to Land Rover Discovery 3/4
problems.rootCause: Early 2.7 TDV6 units (pre-2012) suffer from oil pump housing failure made of brittle cast aluminium. When the pump fails, oil pressure drops instantly, starving the crankshaft bearings. Later Territory-sourced 276DT units have an updated pump design and are considered more reliable than Discovery originals.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace oil pump housing and inspect bearings for damage
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if caught immediately after oil pressure warning; high risk

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt 2.7 TDV6 with upgraded oil pump and bearings
repairOptions.longevity: Best option if engine has knocked or lost pressure - Territory-sourced rebuilds popular with Land Rover owners

problems.recommendation: The 2.7 TDV6 does not give much warning before catastrophic failure. If you hear bottom-end knock or the oil light flickers, stop driving immediately. On a Territory worth £4,000-£6,000, a rebuilt engine from a specialist with upgraded pump components is the only sensible path. Many Land Rover Discovery owners seek Territory donor engines for this very reason.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 276DT engine prices for Ford Territory and Land Rover Discovery

problems.h4: EcoBoost Carbon Buildup - Direct Injection Valve Deposits on 2.0L & 1.5L
problems.affectedModels: Ford Territory SZ MkII (2014-2016, 2.0L EcoBoost CAFDD) / CX743 Chinese production (2018+, 1.5L EcoBoost CAFDB)
problems.rootCause: Direct injection sprays fuel directly into the combustion chamber, bypassing the intake valves. Without fuel washing over them, oil vapours from the PCV system bake onto the valves as hard carbon deposits, restricting airflow and causing misfires .

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Media blasting intake valves to remove carbon deposits
Supply & fit of rebuilt EcoBoost with cleaned/replaced injectors
repairOptions.longevity: Only if misfire has caused valve or piston damage

problems.recommendation: Carbon buildup is maintenance, not failure. If your Territory EcoBoost has rough idle, hesitation, or poor fuel economy, start with a professional intake cleaning. Walnut blasting at a specialist costs a fraction of dealer pricing and typically restores full power. Only consider replacement if you’ve ignored the symptoms for so long that valves are damaged.
problems.cta: Compare EcoBoost carbon cleaning vs rebuilt engine prices for Ford Territory

problems.h4: High Pressure Fuel Pump Failure - Territory Diesel Stalling & Power Loss
problems.affectedModels: Ford Territory SZ (2011-2016, 2.7 TDV6 276DT) / SZ MkII (2014-2016, 2.0L Bi-Turbo CAGDA)
problems.rootCause: The high pressure fuel pump (HPFP) - driven by the engine - develops internal wear, sending metal debris through the fuel system. This contaminates injectors and the fuel rail, leading to stalling, extended cranking, and total loss of power mid-drive .

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace high pressure pump and affected injectors; flush fuel system
repairOptions.longevity: Only if caught before debris spreads to entire system

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt engine with new HPFP, injectors, and fuel system flush
repairOptions.longevity: Required if debris has damaged cylinders or contaminated everything

problems.recommendation: If your diesel Territory stalls at idle or cuts out while driving, don’t keep restarting it - metal debris will only spread. A specialist can test fuel pressure and inspect for contamination. On higher-mileage SZ models (2012-2014), a full rebuilt engine with a new HPFP often makes more financial sense than piecemeal repairs.
problems.cta: Compare Territory diesel HPFP replacement vs rebuilt engine costs

problems.h4: Barra VCT Phaser Failure - Variable Cam Timing Rattle & Poor Performance
problems.affectedModels: Ford Territory SY, SY MkII, SZ (2006-2016, Barra 190 petrol)
problems.rootCause: The Variable Cam Timing (VCT) phaser on the Barra engine uses engine oil pressure to adjust cam timing. When the phaser’s internal locking pin wears or springs fail, the phaser rattles loudly (especially on cold start) and cannot hold correct timing, triggering P0012 or P0016 codes .

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace faulty VCT phaser(s); often done with timing chain kit
repairOptions.longevity: 80,000+ miles if phaser is the only failed component

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt Barra with new VCT phasers and upgraded tensioners
repairOptions.longevity: Better value if timing chain is also worn or engine has jumped timing

problems.recommendation: VCT phaser rattle alone (without performance loss) can be fixed by replacing just the phaser. However, if you’re already paying labour to access the timing cover, fit a complete timing chain kit with phasers - the parts cost is marginal compared to doing the job twice. If the engine has thrown timing correlation codes, go straight to a rebuilt Barra.
problems.cta: Compare Barra VCT phaser repair vs replacement quotes for your Territory

problems.h4: Direct Injection Injector Failure - 2.0L EcoBoost Misfire & Rough Running
problems.affectedModels: Ford Territory SZ MkII (2014-2016, 2.0L EcoBoost CAFDD)
problems.rootCause: Direct injection injectors operate at very high pressures (2,000+ psi). Over time, injector tips can clog, pintles can stick, or internal seals can fail, causing misfires, rough idle, black smoke, and unburnt fuel entering the exhaust .

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace failed injector(s); ECU adaptation reset
repairOptions.longevity: 60,000+ miles if other injectors are healthy

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace all four injectors plus walnut blast intake valves
Supply & fit of rebuilt engine with new injectors and decarbonised head
repairOptions.longevity: Required if misfire has damaged catalytic converter or pistons

problems.recommendation: An injector failure on a 2.0L EcoBoost often presents as a persistent misfire that follows the injector when swapped between cylinders. On a Territory worth £4,000-£6,000, replacing all four injectors plus a walnut blast of the intake valves is the most cost-effective long-term solution. Rebuild only if compression is low or the catalytic converter is destroyed by unburnt fuel.
problems.cta: Compare Territory 2.0 EcoBoost injector replacement vs rebuilt engine prices

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Ford Territory
finalCta.paragraph: A failed engine doesn’t have to mean the end of the road for your Territory. Whether you’re dealing with Barra timing chain rattle, a 2.7 TDV6 oil pump failure, or EcoBoost carbon buildup, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Ford Territory specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components designed to prevent repeat failures, a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty, and nationwide delivery and fitment options. Compare prices today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Ford Territory Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket’s UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Ford Territory and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Ford Territory Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Ford Territory across all generations is listed below, with compatible engine codes, years and common failure notes.

items.title: Ford Territory 2.7L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.7-litre Duratorq V6 turbodiesel (276DT) was introduced with the SZ series in 2011, marking the first diesel option in Territory history. Sourced from Ford's Dagenham plant and shared with Land Rover Discovery 3/4, Jaguar XF and PSA vehicles, this engine offered a 32% fuel economy improvement over the outgoing petrol models while delivering 440Nm of torque from just 1900rpm. Available across TX, TS and Titanium trims in both rear-wheel and all-wheel drive configurations.
compatibleModels: SZ Titanium (2011-2016) - RWD and AWD variants
commonFailurePoints: Oil pump housing failure on early units
items.cta: Get quotes for Ford Territory 2.7L diesel engines

items.title: Ford Territory 1.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.5-litre EcoBoost four-cylinder engine appeared in the second-generation Chinese-built CX743 Territory (2018-2023), representing a significant downsizing from the Australian model's large-displacement heritage. This turbocharged direct-injection unit produces 141hp and 225Nm of torque, paired with a CVT transmission in front-wheel drive configuration. While not officially sold in the UK, some units have been privately imported from markets including the Philippines and the Middle East.
commonFailurePoints: coolant loss from water pump seal
items.cta: Compare Ford Territory 1.5L petrol engine prices

items.title: Ford Territory 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.0-litre EcoBoost joined the Australian-built Territory range with the SZ facelift in 2014, offering V6-like power with improved fuel economy. This turbocharged direct-injection unit produces approximately 240hp and was available in SZ MkII models until production ended in 2016. The second-generation Chinese CX743 Territory also offered a 2.0-litre EcoBoost option in some markets, while the closely related Ford Equator (a Territory successor) uses a 2.0-litre turbocharged unit producing 221hp.
compatibleModels: SZ MkII Titanium EcoBoost (2014-2016); CX743 Territory
commonFailurePoints: Carbon buildup on intake valves (direct injection)
items.cta: Compare Ford Territory 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Ford Territory 4.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The legendary 4.0-litre Barra inline-six was the sole engine powering first-generation SX (2004-2006) and SY (2006-2011) Territory models, and continued alongside the diesel in early SZ series (2011-2014) RWD variants. This DOHC 24-valve engine is renowned for exceptional reliability and 400,000km+ lifespans in Australian conditions. Early SX models produced 182kW (244hp) and 380Nm, upgraded to 190kW (250hp) and 383Nm for SY and early SZ variants. The final SZ petrol iteration (2011-2014) reached 195kW (261hp) and 391Nm. Drive configuration determines transmission: four-speed auto for RWD SX/SY models; six-speed ZF 6HP26 for AWD models.
items.cta: Compare Ford Territory 4.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Ford Territory 1.8L Petrol Engines (Markets outside UK)
items.description: The 1.8-litre EcoBoost four-cylinder engine (4G18F6E) appears in the second-generation Territory for markets including the Americas and Middle East. This turbocharged direct-injection unit represents the modern downsized direction for the nameplate, offered alongside the 1.5L and 2.0L EcoBoost options in various markets.
items.productionYears: Americas and Middle East markets only - not officially sold in UK
commonFailurePoints: Limited UK data - typical EcoBoost direct-injection carbon buildup expected
items.cta: Compare Ford Territory 1.8L petrol engine prices

engineYears.h2: Ford Territory Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: From the 2004 SX launch with the legendary Barra petrol inline-six to the 2011 SZ introduction of the Land Rover-sourced 2.7L diesel, this timeline covers every major engine change across the Territory's Australian production run (2004-2016).

years.description: The Ford Territory launched in April 2004, sharing the BA Falcon's platform and 4.0L Barra inline-six - but with SUV practicality that would eventually spell the end for the Falcon wagon.
keyChanges: First and only SUV ever produced in Australia
Based on BA Falcon platform with rear-wheel and all-wheel drive options
4.0L Barra 182 petrol engine carried over from Falcon
Four-speed automatic transmission (ION) across all models
Available in TX, TS and Ghia trim levels
knownFor: Award-winning SUV that redefined Australian family transport
Spacious interior and BMW-rivalling dynamics
Thirsty fuel consumption - the main driver for diesel demand later
Exceptional refinement for a locally-built SUV
years.cta: Get quotes for 2004-2005 Ford Territory replacement engines

years.description: Ford introduced the turbocharged Barra to the Territory range, bringing XR6T levels of performance to the SUV segment.
keyChanges: Turbocharged 4.0L Barra added to AWD models
Six-speed ZF automatic gearbox introduced on AWD Turbo models
Turbo models identifiable by bonnet scoop and dual exhaust outlets
Power increased to 245kW and 480Nm
mainEngines: Barra 245T - 4.0L turbo petrol (AWD Turbo, Ghia Turbo)
knownFor: Turbo Barra delivers effortless overtaking and towing
FPV F6X (2008) later pushed output to 270kW/550Nm
Turbo models remain sought-after by enthusiasts
Six-speed ZF gearbox a major refinement upgrade
years.cta: Get quotes for 2005-2006 Ford Territory Turbo replacement engines

years.preview: SY Update: Power Boost & New Gearbox
years.description: The SY Territory arrived in October 2005 with visual changes limited to colours and trim, but significant mechanical upgrades including power increase and six-speed ZF for AWD models.
keyChanges: Barra power increased from 182kW to 190kW (383Nm torque)
Six-speed ZF automatic fitted to all AWD models
RWD models retained four-speed ION transmission
Reversing camera added as option
SR variant introduced as value-added model (2006)
knownFor: Barra 190 remains the most common Territory engine
Legendary reliability - many exceed 400,000km
Timing chain stretch with hydraulic tensioner failure - key replacement driver
ZF six-speed transformed AWD driving experience
years.cta: Get quotes for SY Ford Territory replacement engines
years.ticker: Barra timing chain rattle • P0016 cam/crank correlation code • VCT phaser failure on SY Territory
enquiries: VCT phaser failure on SY Territory

years.description: Ford Performance Vehicles (FPV) released the F6X - the most powerful Territory ever produced, sharing its 270kW/550Nm engine with the FG Falcon F6.
keyChanges: FPV F6X launched with 4.0L turbo Barra producing 270kW/550Nm
Exclusive to AWD configuration
Unique suspension tuning and performance brakes
Limited production run - rare and collectible
knownFor: Most powerful Australian-built SUV ever produced
Extremely rare - low production numbers
Highly sought after by collectors and enthusiasts
Performance parts still available through specialist channels
years.cta: Get quotes for FPV F6X Territory replacement engines

years.description: The May 2009 SY MkII brought a visual facelift with new nose design, bonnet, headlights and grille - while the Barra engine received minor efficiency improvements.
keyChanges: New front styling (bonnet, headlights, grille, bumper)
Revised tail-lights and side mouldings
New interior trim options (Shadow charcoal or Cashmere cream)
Improved fuel consumption via new catalytic converter and software
TS Limited Edition variant available
knownFor: SY MkII refined the successful Barra platform
Limited Edition TS models popular with value buyers
Last of the pre-diesel Territory range
Petrol-only range until SZ arrival in 2011

years.description: The 2011 SZ Territory transformed the range with the long-awaited 2.7L V6 diesel from Land Rover Discovery, capturing 72-75% of sales within its first year and reviving the model's fortunes.
keyChanges: 2.7L Duratorq V6 diesel (276DT) introduced - 140kW/440Nm
Diesel available in TX, TS and Titanium trims (RWD and AWD)
Petrol Barra now RWD only - AWD reserved for diesel
Petrol Barra upgraded to 195kW/391Nm
Six-speed 6R80 auto for diesel (shared with F-Series truck)
ZF six-speed retained for petrol models
popularModels: Ford Territory TX (petrol RWD / diesel RWD/AWD)
Ford Territory TS (petrol RWD / diesel RWD/AWD)
knownFor: Diesel engine shared with Land Rover Discovery 3/4 and Jaguar XF
32% fuel economy improvement over outgoing petrol
2700kg towing capacity on diesel AWD (400kg more than petrol)
Oil pump housing failure on early diesel units (updated later)
Petrol Barra now RWD-only - enthusiasts mourn AWD petrol loss

years.ticker: 2.7 TDV6 oil pump failure • 276DT crankshaft bearing wear • Discovery 3 donor engine from Territory

years.preview: SZ MkII: EcoBoost Joins the Range
years.description: The SZ facelift added a third engine option - the 2.0L EcoBoost petrol - alongside the Barra and diesel, offering buyers a choice of three powertrains in the Territory's final years.
keyChanges: 2.0L EcoBoost (CAFDD) added - 240hp turbo petrol
EcoBoost offered improved fuel economy over Barra
2.0L Bi-Turbo diesel (CAGDA) also available in some markets
SZ facelift exterior styling refresh
Final Australian-built Territory rolled off line October 2016

Over 105,000 units sold across SX/SY generations before SZ

Diesel remained most popular - over 70% of SZ sales
Territory's success contributed to Falcon wagon's demise in 2010

faq.h2: Ford Territory Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Ford Territory engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Rebuilt Ford Territory engine prices range from £1,400 to £2,800 depending on engine code, with 2.7L diesel (276DT) at £1,950-£2,800 and 4.0L Barra petrol from £1,200-£2,800. Supply & fit adds £400-£700.
keyPoints: 2.7L V6 diesel (276DT): £1,950-£2,800 for rebuilt unit
4.0L Barra (190/182): £1,200-£2,800 depending on mileage and condition
2.0L EcoBoost (CAFDD): £1,400-£2,200 rebuilt
Budget £2,400-£3,500 total with professional fitting
Reconditioned units include 12-month warranty vs used with no warranty
items.cta: Compare Ford Territory engine prices by code from UK specialists

items.question: Does the Ford Territory 4.0L Barra have a timing belt or chain?
items.answer: The Ford Territory 4.0L Barra petrol engine has a timing chain, not a belt. There is no routine timing belt replacement schedule. The chain is designed to last the engine's life when properly maintained with regular oil changes.
keyPoints: Barra uses timing chain with hydraulic tensioner - no belt to replace
No factory service interval for chain replacement
Chain stretch causes P0016 fault code and cold start rattle
Regular oil changes (every 10,000-15,000km) critical for chain life
Replacement chain kit includes guides, tensioner and sprockets
items.cta: Get quotes for Ford Territory Barra timing chain replacement

items.question: Is the Ford Territory 2.7 diesel engine reliable?
items.answer: SZ Territory 2.7 TDV6 (276DT) engines are considered more reliable than Land Rover Discovery units of the same era, but early production (2011-2012) suffered oil pump housing failures. Later versions have updated components.
keyPoints: Updated oil pump on later Territory diesels - less prone to failure
Shared with Discovery 3/4 - Territory engines sought as donor units
Typical failure 140,000-220,000km if oil pump fails
One reported spun bearing at just 60,000km - likely build issue
Turbo lag common complaint but not a failure point
items.cta: Compare rebuilt 276DT engine prices for Ford Territory

items.question: What are the most common Ford Territory engine problems?
items.answer: Barra petrol engines suffer timing chain stretch and VCT phaser rattle. 2.7 diesel units have oil pump housing failures. EcoBoost engines develop carbon buildup on intake valves due to direct injection design.
keyPoints: Barra timing chain rattle: P0016 code, hydraulic tensioner failure
Barra VCT phaser failure: rattle on cold start, poor low-end performance
2.7TDV6 oil pump failure: brittle cast housing on early SZ models
2.0 EcoBoost carbon buildup: walnut blasting required every 40-60k miles
Diff bushes and auto transmission also common failure points
items.cta: Find Ford Territory engine specialists near you

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in my Ford Territory?
items.answer: Replacing a Territory engine is worth it if the body and transmission are sound. A rebuilt Barra or 276DT with 12-month warranty costs £1,950-£2,800 - less than replacing an equivalent SUV. Barra engines regularly exceed 400,000km when rebuilt properly.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engine cost £1,400-£2,800 plus fitting
Comparable used SUV costs £5,000-£10,000
Barra legendary reliability after rebuild - 400,000km+ possible
Spun bearing repair often exceeds engine replacement cost
SZ diesel models hold value well due to Discovery donor demand
items.cta: Get a Ford Territory replacement engine quote today

items.question: Which Ford Territory engine should I avoid?
items.answer: Early 2011-2012 SZ 2.7 diesel engines have oil pump housing failures - check if updated pump was fitted. For Barra petrol, avoid neglected examples with no service history - timing chain issues are inevitable. EcoBoost requires regular carbon cleaning.
keyPoints: Avoid pre-2012 2.7 diesel without updated oil pump documentation
Barra with timing rattle needs immediate chain replacement
High-mileage EcoBoost with no walnut blast history will have carbon issues
Turbo Barra (245T) more complex - higher running costs
Well-maintained Barra petrol is safest long-term choice
items.cta: Check Ford Territory engine availability by code

items.question: How long does a Ford Territory Barra engine last?
items.answer: A well-maintained Ford Territory Barra 4.0L petrol engine regularly exceeds 400,000km (250,000 miles). The legendary Australian inline-six is known for exceptional durability when serviced with correct oil and timing chain tensioner maintenance.
keyPoints: 400,000km+ common in Australian taxis and fleets
Timing chain and tensioner need attention around 200,000km
Regular 10,000km oil changes critical for VCT phaser health
Thirsty but reliable - fuel economy is main complaint
Turbo Barra slightly less durable due to added complexity

items.question: What is the fuel consumption of Ford Territory engines?
items.answer: Barra 4.0L petrol averages 13L/100km (21.7mpg) around town, 10.6L/100km claimed. 2.7 diesel is far more economical at 8.2L/100km (34.4mpg) for RWD, 8.8L/100km for AWD - the main reason diesel dominated SZ sales.
keyPoints: 4.0L Barra petrol: 13L/100km real-world, 10.6L/100km claimed
2.7 TDV6 diesel RWD: 8.2L/100km claimed
2.7 TDV6 diesel AWD: 8.8L/100km claimed
Diesel captured 72-75% of SZ sales due to economy
32% fuel saving switching from petrol to diesel
items.cta: Get quotes for Ford Territory diesel engine replacement

items.question: Does a Land Rover Discovery engine fit a Ford Territory?
items.answer: Yes, the 2.7L TDV6 diesel (276DT) is identical between Ford Territory SZ (2011-2016) and Land Rover Discovery 3/4. Territory-sourced units are often preferred as donor engines because they're considered more reliable than Discovery originals.
keyPoints: Same PSA/Lion V6 engine - fully interchangeable
Territory 276DT engines have updated oil pump vs early Discovery
Land Rover owners actively seek Territory donor engines
Check engine code 276DT for compatibility
Specialist Land Rover garages can fit Territory engines
items.cta: Compare 276DT engine prices for Ford Territory and Land Rover

items.question: What engine codes were fitted to the Ford Territory?
items.answer: Ford Territory engine codes: Barra 182/190/195 (4.0L petrol, SX/SY/SZ 2004-2014), 276DT (2.7 V6 diesel, SZ 2011-2016), CAFDD (2.0 EcoBoost, SZ MkII 2014-2016), and CAFDB/CAGDA (1.5/2.0 EcoBoost, CX743 Chinese production 2018-2023).
keyPoints: Barra 182/190/195: 4.0L petrol - most common UK import
276DT (Duratorq): 2.7 V6 diesel - shared with Discovery 3/4
CAFDD: 2.0 EcoBoost petrol - 240hp, facelift only
CAFDB: 1.5L EcoBoost - CX743 Chinese market
CAGDA: 2.0L Bi-Turbo diesel - CX743 only
items.cta: Identify your Ford Territory engine code with registration lookup
